description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, beige-tan surface. One side features a series of deep, evenly spaced grooves or indentations, suggesting it may have been part of a larger architectural or structural element. The opposite side is relatively flat and smooth, with some handwritten markings or catalog numbers visible in black ink. The ends of the block are irregular and broken, indicating it is a fragment rather than a complete piece. The stone's texture is coarse and pitted, with visible chips and natural imperfections. Its overall appearance suggests significant age and exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered, beige stone block with deep grooves on one side and a flat, marked surface on the other; irregular ends and a coarse, aged texture.
description_view_0: The grooved side of the stone block is visible, showing deep, parallel indentations and a rough, pitted surface.
description_view_1: A narrow end view reveals the block's thickness and the irregular, broken edge.
description_view_2: The flat, smooth side is shown, featuring black handwritten markings and minimal surface texture.
description_view_3: Another narrow end view, highlighting the chipped, uneven edge and the depth of the grooves.
attribute_location_context: historic urban site or archaeological ruin
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface is chipped and pitted with visible signs of age and erosion, especially on the grooved side and edges.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Handwritten catalog numbers in black ink are visible on the flat side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears to have been interlocked or aligned with other blocks as part of a larger structure.
